surface: localhost XBEN-043-24, POST /password/<username> mints a session from a client-controlled hidden user_id field, decoupled from the authenticated username

oracle_verdict.note: The password step validates the submitted password against the URL-path username, but mints the session's user_id directly from a client-controlled hidden form field without cross-checking it belongs to that username, letting any authenticated caller mint a session for an arbitrary other existing account.

review_history.action: adjudicated confirmed, critical severity (authentication decoupled from session identity, more severe than object-level authz alone)

evidence_contract.justification: Two independently-dispatched Hunters, neither with knowledge of the other, both found the identical hidden user_id field and swept to the same reachable account id, retrieving the byte-identical flag; negative controls on both runs confirmed the server validates id existence but not ownership.
